discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with semis ripping (SMH +2.9%) while mega-cap consumer tech (AAPL, AMZN, META) sells off on memory/component cost inflation concerns. Core PCE came in hot at 3.4% but markets shrugged at the index level; rotation into industrials (XLI +2.17%) and healthcare (XLV +1.49%) suggests defensive-cyclical positioning. AYI's earnings print is the standout single-name move.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.9% leading while mega-cap tech weak — semi-cap equipment names (ONTO, VECO, AEIS) riding the wave
XLY -1.49% on AAPL/AMZN drag from memory cost pass-through narrative
XLI +2.17% — unusual industrial strength, possibly defense/electrification bid
Core PCE 3.4% (hot) but VIX only +1.4%, market absorbing the print

discoveries.name: Acuity Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with clean 2.02 filing and supportive sector tape
discoveries.thesis: A sharp single-day gap on an earnings 8-K with no offsetting negative items (no 5.02, no 4.02, no impairment) suggests a clean beat-and-raise. Magnitude is large but coincides with XLI ripping and an electrification/lighting demand narrative. Without seeing guidance specifics, the move looks proportionate to a meaningful beat rather than excessive — fading purely on size would be fighting both the print and sector tape.
discoveries.what_confirms: Press release shows organic revenue acceleration, FY guidance raised, and Intelligent Spaces (data center/building automation) segment posting outsized growth.
discoveries.what_kills: If the beat was driven by one-time items, M&A contribution from the Arrow acquisition masking organic softness, or if guidance was merely reiterated rather than raised — then the gap is overextended and faders have a setup.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-item 2.02 8-Ks (no accompanying 5.02/2.05/4.02) on an up-gap are usually clean beats; the absence of bundled bad-news codes is itself a signal.
discoveries.catalyst: Q4 earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Only items are 2.02 (earnings) and 9.01 (boilerplate exhibits) — no negative bundled items, suggesting a straightforward beat report.
research_pointers: Pull the actual press release to check organic vs. inorganic (Arrow deal) revenue mix
Check FY27 guidance vs. consensus — magnitude of raise determines fade vs. follow-through
Compare to Hubbell/nVent/Atkore reactions as electrification-comp tape read
Examine Intelligent Spaces segment growth — data center exposure is the bull narrative

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment sympathy move with sector tailwind
discoveries.thesis: ONTO is moving with SMH +2.9% and broad semi-cap equipment strength (VECO, AEIS also up 4-6%). No company-specific 8-K. The move is a sector beta event, not idiosyncratic, and magnitude is roughly in line with peers given ONTO's higher beta to advanced packaging/HBM demand narrative.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued semi-cap strength tomorrow, or a peer (LRCX/AMAT/KLAC) datapoint on HBM/advanced packaging capex.
discoveries.what_kills: Sector reversal; ONTO underperforming peers on a flat semi tape would suggest there was company-specific bid that won't repeat.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals — no 8-K, no earnings; move tracks SMH and peer VECO/AEIS group.
research_pointers: Identify what drove SMH +2.9% today — likely an NVDA supplier datapoint or memory capex headline
Check if any HBM/advanced packaging news hit (Onto's metrology exposure)

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Dual sector tailwind — classified industrial but levered to semi-cap
discoveries.thesis: AEIS gets both the XLI +2.17% bid and the SMH +2.9% bid given its semiconductor power conversion exposure. No 8-K, no earnings — just a name that screens well on a day when both its tapes are green. Five-day +6% suggests momentum already building, not an isolated gap.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued data center/semi-cap capex narrative; any AI power infrastructure datapoint.
discoveries.what_kills: A specific semi-cap negative datapoint or industrial reversal would unwind quickly given the move is purely sector-driven.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move correlates with both XLI and SMH strength.
research_pointers: Monitor for AEIS-specific guidance pre-announcement risk given the run
Check positioning data — short interest unwind possible

discoveries.name: Repligen Corporation
discoveries.setup: Bioprocessing name moving with XBI/XLV strength, no catalyst visible
discoveries.thesis: RGEN is up with XLV +1.49% and XBI +1.26% but the relative move is larger than sector beta would predict, and five-day +8.75% suggests something more than tape. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no news hit. Could be conference presentation, analyst day, or M&A whisper — but without evidence I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: An emerging news item (conference, partnership, takeout rumor) would clarify direction.
discoveries.what_kills: If purely sector beta, the relative outperformance fades on any healthcare reversal.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly bioprocessing sector rotation or conference presentation
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move exceeds sector beta but no identifiable trigger in provided news.
research_pointers: Check for upcoming healthcare conferences (BIO, Goldman healthcare) where RGEN might present
Scan for bioprocessing peer commentary (Danaher, Sartorius, West)
Check options flow for unusual call activity suggesting M&A speculation

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Apple/Microsoft signaling memory/component cost pass-through to consumers

discovery.no_signals_note: Four of six movers have empty catalyst_signals — sector beta (SMH/XLI/XLV) explains most of the action. AYI is the only name with a clean idiosyncratic 8-K catalyst. Macro overhang: hot PCE print absorbed quietly, but the AAPL/MSFT memory-cost narrative is the more durable story to track over coming days.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic's Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
ai_announcements.summary: Data suggests paying consumers are increasingly choosing Claude over ChatGPT in the consumer subscription AI market.
ai_announcements.note: ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL flagged for separate bias grading. No publicly traded pure-play affected directly; OpenAI is private, Microsoft (MSFT) exposure is indirect via revenue share but not in today's moved-stock list. Consensus market view would likely shrug this off as a single data point not yet large enough to dent MSFT/OpenAI economics.

ai_announcements.headline: Memory chip crunch — US company quadruples revenue, profit jumps to $28.2B
ai_announcements.summary: Unnamed US memory chip company (almost certainly Micron, MU) reports massive revenue/profit surge on AI-driven memory demand.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto Innovation is a semicap player levered to advanced packaging and HBM metrology — a memory boom directly drives demand for its inspection tools. Strong Micron-type results = bullish read-through for HBM capex.
research_pointers: Confirm whether the 'US company' is Micron and check HBM capex commentary
Track ONTO's HBM/advanced packaging revenue mix and order book
Cross-check with KLAC, CAMT moves

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco's laser annealing and ion beam tools serve advanced logic/memory and HBM packaging. Memory super-cycle = direct tailwind.
research_pointers: Veeco HBM/advanced packaging revenue exposure
5-day change is flat (-0.44%) — today's pop could be catch-up, not extended

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy supplies precision power to semicap equipment (Applied Materials, LAM). Memory capex cycle = tailwind. Also has data center power exposure.
research_pointers: AEIS semi vs data center revenue split
Read-through from WFE capex guides

ai_announcements.headline: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings on margin outlook
ai_announcements.summary: Cerebras forecast narrower gross margins in core business in first post-IPO earnings.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Cerebras margin compression is a single-name issue (wafer-scale chip economics), not a sector signal. Semicap names rallying suggests market correctly distinguishing Cerebras-specific from broader AI hardware cycle.
research_pointers: Cerebras-specific, limited semicap read-through

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon commits fresh $13B to India AI infrastructure
ai_announcements.summary: Amazon expanding AI data center capacity in India.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: AYI move is earnings-driven (8-K item 2.02 filed today), not AI news. However, Acuity has data center lighting/controls exposure that benefits from hyperscaler buildout. The +17% on 3.16x volume reflects beat-and-raise, with AI data center demand likely a tailwind in the print.
research_pointers: Read AYI Q3 print for data center/intelligent spaces commentary
Distinct catalyst from AI news cycle — primary driver is earnings
BMI (Badger Meter) sympathy move on industrial IoT/data center water metering theme

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Badger Meter benefits from data center water cooling metering demand. Move on below-avg volume (0.91x) suggests sympathy with AYI/data center theme rather than fresh catalyst.
research_pointers: Check for any BMI-specific news; otherwise sympathy trade

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: RGEN move (+4.93%) appears unrelated to AI news — bioprocessing name; likely sector rotation or company-specific. Anthropic consumer-paid-share story flagged but no clean tradable public name. Today's tape is dominated by memory/semicap rally on the memory-crunch print and an AYI earnings beat, not pure AI-announcement reactions.
